cards.name: Alfred the Great

abilities.name: Scelden Feohte
abilities.text: At the start of each round, name an ability that gives you a bonus to an attack roll for that round. That ability gives all of your defense rolls a +1 bonus instead of the attack roll bonus.

cards.flavor: In addition to repeatedly defeating the invading Danish Vikings, King Alfred the Great pushed for better education and help to make learning important in the lives of his people. He is the only English king to ever be given the title of "the Great."
cards.background:
Alfred the Great is said to have been the first true king of the English. Pressed further and further into the desolate Athelney marshes, Alfred and his soldiers led a counter-offensive against the Danish invaders that succeeded in winning back much of their previous lands. Alfred’s enlightened rule and his establishment of town defenses and regular militia ensured lasting safety for his people.

Wielding the traditional longsword, Alfred fights for the lives of his people and the lasting devotion of his wife Ealswith. This noble and sturdy warrior commands respect from his allies and opponents alike.
cards.raw_source_text: Scelden Feohte: At the start of each round, name an ability that gives you a bonus to an attack roll for that round. That ability gives all of your defense rolls a +1 bonus instead of the attack roll bonus.

cards.name: Ealswith

abilities.name: For de Cwen
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each point of damage dealt to you during the last round.

cards.flavor: Married to King Alfred in Winchester, she ruled by his side until his death, after which she took holy vows and became a nun. Her son, Edward, went on to rule England.
cards.raw_source_text: For de Cwen: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each point of damage dealt to you during the last round.

cards.name: Longsword

abilities.name: Angra Feohte
abilities.text: If you hit an opposing warrior with this weapon, that warrior gets -2 experience for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: The primary postures or ready positions for long-swords are Ochs, Pflug, Alber and Vom Tag. These are; the Ox, the Plow, the Fool, the Roof. These correspond to: in the middle ("Plow"), outside high/horizontal pointing ("Ox"), low ("Fool"), and high ("Roof").
cards.raw_source_text: Angra Feohte: If you hit an opposing warrior with this weapon, that warrior gets -2 experience for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Gambeson

abilities.name: Strican Helpe
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 against bludgeon and basic attacks.

cards.flavor: Serving as a support, the gambeson spreads the weight of the overlaying hauberk much more evenly on the shoulders. The gambeson also assures that the rings of the hauberk stay at a safe distance from the skin, avoiding nasty wounds in combat. Finally, the gambeson acts as a custion to absorb blows.
cards.raw_source_text: Strican Helpe: Your defense rolls gain +2 against bludgeon and basic attacks.

cards.name: Targe

abilities.name: Agaenes-feohte
abilities.text: When you are attacked and missed, your next attack roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: The targe, or target, was a traditional, round shield, made of wood and covered in leather. Later versions combined steel with wood. It was primarily used to parry attacks aimed at the body.
cards.raw_source_text: Agaenes-feohte: When you are attacked and missed, your next attack roll gains +2.

cards.name: Boudicca

abilities.name: Swift Swidnes
abilities.text: While you have a face up cavalry card, your attack rolls gain +1 and your support cards gain +2 initiative.

cards.flavor: After she and her two daughters were gravely humiliated by the Romans, Boudicca led a revolt of the Iceni and several other Celtic tribes against the Roman empire. Her forces burned and destroyed the three major towns of Londinium, Verulamium and Camulodunum before being defeated by the Roman military governor, Suetonius Paullinus.
cards.background:
With the death of her husband, King Prasutagus, the lives of Boudicca’s people changed forever. Breaking vows to her husband and people, Roman centurions enslaved her family, destroyed her home and ravished her daughters. In retribution, Boudicca led a rebellion against the Romans that cut a swath of destruction across the land.

Boudicca rides into battle atop a swift carpat chariot and prosecutes her war of vengeance at the point of a goaf spear. Driven by an unquenchable desire to topple her enemies, Boudicca is a formidable adversary in the Anachronism™ arena.
cards.raw_source_text: Swift Swidnes: While you have a face up cavalry card, your attack rolls gain +1 and your support cards gain +2 initiative.

cards.name: Rhiannon

abilities.name: Wintsang
abilities.text: Each time a cavalry card is revealed (including this one), you gain one life.

cards.flavor: Goddess of birds and horses, Rhiannon was unjustly accused of killing her newborn son. She silently accepted her punishment to carry guests from the gate into the castle on her back. When the fiend that had kidnapped her son was killed and her son returned, she was returned her place as queen.
cards.raw_source_text: Wintsang: Each time a cavalry card is revealed (including this one), you gain one life.

cards.name: Rhiannon

abilities.name: Wintsang
abilities.text: Each time a cavalry card is revealed (including this one), you gain one life.

cards.flavor: Goddess of birds and horses, Rhiannon was unjustly accused of killing her newborn son. She silently accepted her punishment to carry guests from the gate into the castle on her back. When the fiend that had kidnapped her son was killed and her son returned, she was returned her place as queen.
cards.raw_source_text: Wintsang: Each time a cavalry card is revealed (including this one), you gain one life.

cards.name: Goaf

abilities.name: Haeft Strican
abilities.text: After you make a basic attack, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: One of the oldest and most important of all hand weapons, the Celts of Britain favored the spear as their main armament. The goaf was primarily used from the back of a chariot.
cards.raw_source_text: Haeft Strican: After you make a basic attack, you may move one space.

cards.name: Bratt

abilities.name: Tangilen
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 against polearms and ranged weapons.

cards.flavor: A coarse woolen cloak, the bratt was an indispensable garment to any norther Celt. Large enough to double as a blanket when sleeping outdoors, it could be worn long and sweeping for warmth and could be folded and draped for ease of movement.
cards.raw_source_text: Tangilen: Your defense rolls gain +2 against polearms and ranged weapons.

cards.name: Carpat

abilities.name: Sipe
abilities.text: Once per round, if you are adjacent to an opposing warrior and there is an empty space on the opposite side of them, move to that space and deal 2 damage to them.

cards.flavor: With its scythed wheels and light, fast build, the Carpat was capable of causing great damage to infantry. It was Boudicca's greatest weapon against the Roman soldiers.
cards.raw_source_text: Sipe - 2 Actions: Once per round, if you are adjacent to an opposing warrior and there is an empty space on the opposite side of them, move to that space and deal 2 damage to them.

cards.name: King Arthur

abilities.name: Poer of de Stan
abilities.text: During the first round of the game, your attack rolls gain +3.

cards.flavor:
The personality of Arthur is unknown and unknowable. But he was a real as Alfred the Great or William the Conqueror; his impact upon future ages mattered as much, or more so.
-John Morris/The Age of Arthur
cards.background:
More than just legend, a king named Arthur fought so bravely against the Saxons that plagued his people that they were driven to the north end of England. The 50-year respite England enjoyed from these attacks is attributed to Arthur’s courage and prowess. That Arthur’s story has grown to become so central in western legend is a testament to his greatness.

Arthur bears the gleaming sword Excalibur, gifted to him by the Lady of the Lake. Aided by the mysterious figure of Merlin, Arthur now lays claim to the title of highest and best just as he did in ages past. Wield the power of legend to gain victory in the Anachronism™ arena!
cards.raw_source_text: Poer of de Stan: During the first round of the game, your attack rolls gain +3.

cards.name: Lady of the Lake

abilities.name: Epiphanes
abilities.text: Name a reveal ability on any other face up card. Use that ability as if you just revealed that card.

cards.flavor: Known for presenting the gleaming Excalibur to Arthur, the Lady of the Lake, Vivienne, was also a student and lover of Arthur's mentor, Merlin.
cards.raw_source_text: Epiphanes - Reveal: Name a reveal ability on any other face up card. Use that ability as if you just revealed that card.

cards.name: Excalibur

abilities.name: Mirouren Blaed
abilities.text: For this round, this weapon deals additional damage equal to the base damage of an opponent's weapon.

cards.flavor: Sword of legend and kings, whoever held Excalibur ruled supreme.
cards.raw_source_text: Mirouren Blaed - Reveal: For this round, this weapon deals additional damage equal to the base damage of an opponent's weapon.

cards.name: Chainmail Shirt

abilities.name: Weardian de Ende
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 against sword and ranged weapons.

cards.flavor: Interlocking iron or steel rings easily turned aside the edge or point of any bladed weapon, Instead of slicing the skin, the wearer would often escape with little more than a bruise.
cards.raw_source_text: Weardian de Ende: Your defense rolls gain +2 against sword and ranged weapons.

cards.name: Merlin the Wise

abilities.name: Aector of de Drakon
abilities.text: Before rolling, discard this card and another face up, non-weapon support cards. If you do, that roll gains +3.

cards.flavor: Attributed with deeds ranging from elevating Arthur to kingship to the construction of Stonehenge, Merlin presided over King Arthur's kingdom.
cards.raw_source_text: Aector of de Drakon: Before rolling, discard this card and another face up, non-weapon support cards. If you do, that roll gains +3.

cards.name: Richard the Lionheart

abilities.name: Fixe Strican
abilities.text: After you miss an attack, your next attack roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: Also know as "The Absent King," during Richard the Lionheart's ten year reign as the King of England, he spent less than six months in England. The rest of his reign was spent fighting in France, Cyprus and in the Third Crusade against the Sarcacen leader Saladin..
cards.background:
Warrior, crusader, king – Richard the Lionheart spent much of his life on the battlefield. Rarely tarrying for any length of time in his own kingdom, Richard’s few stays in England were for the sole purposes of recruiting and supplying new armies. Unquestionably brave on the battlefield, Richard acquired a profound respect for his opponents during the Crusades.

Armored in a full mail hauberk and wielding the powerful greatsword, Richard is an implacable foe in the Anachronism™ arena. Guided by the tenets of chivalry, all who face him will come to know why he is called “The Lionheart”.
cards.raw_source_text: Fixe Strican: After you miss an attack, your next attack roll gains +2.

cards.name: Richard the Lionheart

abilities.name: Fixe Strican
abilities.text: After you miss an attack, your next attack roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: Also know as "The Absent King," during Richard the Lionheart's ten year reign as the King of England, he spent less than six months in England. The rest of his reign was spent fighting in France, Cyprus and in the Third Crusade against the Sarcacen leader Saladin..
cards.background:
Warrior, crusader, king – Richard the Lionheart spent much of his life on the battlefield. Rarely tarrying for any length of time in his own kingdom, Richard’s few stays in England were for the sole purposes of recruiting and supplying new armies. Unquestionably brave on the battlefield, Richard acquired a profound respect for his opponents during the Crusades.

Armored in a full mail hauberk and wielding the powerful greatsword, Richard is an implacable foe in the Anachronism™ arena. Guided by the tenets of chivalry, all who face him will come to know why he is called “The Lionheart”.
cards.raw_source_text: Fixe Strican: After you miss an attack, your next attack roll gains +2.

cards.name: Bernegnaria

abilities.name: Latost Freondlufu
abilities.text: At the end of this round, gain an amount of life equal to the number of times you hit another warrior this round.

cards.flavor: Though Richard refused marriage for many years, he relented to Bernegnaria after conquering the isle of Cyprus. They were wed there, making Berengaria Queen of England and Cyprus, Duchess of Normandy, and Countess of Anjou.
cards.raw_source_text: Latost Freondlufu - Reveal: At the end of this round, gain an amount of life equal to the number of times you hit another warrior this round.

cards.name: Greatsword

abilities.name: Ridon dem Dune
abilities.text: This weapon deals +1 damage while you have a face up cavalry card.

cards.flavor: The English greatsword was heavy and difficult to maneuver, though with the distinct advantage of crushing anything it hit. When pitted against the scimitars of the Saracens in the Crusades it was found to be slow and easily evaded by the more lightly clad knights of the desert.
cards.raw_source_text: Ridon dem Dune: This weapon deals +1 damage while you have a face up cavalry card.

cards.name: Full Mail Hauberk

abilities.name: Mast Laestan
abilities.text: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Developed to withstand blows from a charging knight, full mail was cumbersome and unweildy, but effective.
cards.raw_source_text: Mast Laestan: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.name: Full Mail Hauberk

abilities.name: Mast Laestan
abilities.text: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Developed to withstand blows from a charging knight, full mail was cumbersome and unweildy, but effective.
cards.raw_source_text: Mast Laestan: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.name: Full Mail Hauberk

abilities.name: Mast Laestan
abilities.text: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Developed to withstand blows from a charging knight, full mail was cumbersome and unweildy, but effective.
cards.raw_source_text: Mast Laestan: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.name: Full Mail Hauberk

abilities.name: Mast Laestan
abilities.text: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Developed to withstand blows from a charging knight, full mail was cumbersome and unweildy, but effective.
cards.raw_source_text: Mast Laestan: You suffer 1 less damage from all attacks (except ranged attacks), to a minimum of 1 damage.

cards.name: Chivalry

abilities.name: A Cnihte Honur
abilities.text: Each round, if you win initiative, you may choose to go second. If you do, your attack and defense rolls this round gain +1.

cards.flavor: Thou shalt respect all weaknesses, and shalt constitute thyself the defender of them.
cards.raw_source_text: A Cnihte Honur: Each round, if you win initiative, you may choose to go second. If you do, your attack and defense rolls this round gain +1.

cards.name: Robin Hood

abilities.name: Treowe Ame
abilities.text: You gain +3 experience and your attack rolls gain +1 if you have a ranged weapon.

cards.flavor: Becoming the basis of many legends, the earliest Robin Hood was a yeoman, not a wronged nobleman, who haunted Barnsdale Forest.
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Treowe Ame: You gain +3 experience and your attack rolls gain +1 if you have a ranged weapon.

cards.name: Maid Marion

abilities.name: Kidnapen
abilities.text: Place this card in any space in the arena.

abilities.name: Rescue
abilities.text: Move to this card's space.

cards.flavor:
A bonny fine maid of noble degree,
Maid Marion callled by name,
Did live in the North, of excellent worth,
For she was a gallant dame."
-Traditional Folk Song
cards.raw_source_text:
Kidnapen - Reveal: Place this card in any space in the arena. 
Rescue - Action: Move to this card's space.

cards.name: Long Bow

abilities.name: Percen Feohte
abilities.text: If there are two empty ranks or columns between you and the defending warrior, this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: During the Middle Ages, the national weapon of the English army was the longbow. It was this weapon which conquered Wales and Scotland, gave the English their victories in the Hundred Years War and permitted England to replace France as the foremost military power in Europe.
cards.raw_source_text: Percen Feohte: If there are two empty ranks or columns between you and the defending warrior, this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Leather Jerkin

abilities.name: Tohte Hyd
abilities.text: Your defense roll gains +1 the first time you are attacked each round. Before you make a defense roll, you may discard this card. If you do, your defense roll gains +4.

cards.flavor: The leather Jerkin, a military garment adapted for civilian use, was also known as buff jerkin, as it was made of buff, an oxhide dressed with oil.
cards.raw_source_text: Tohte Hyd: Your defense roll gains +1 the first time you are attacked each round. Before you make a defense roll, you may discard this card. If you do, your defense roll gains +4.

cards.name: Band of Merry Men

abilities.name: Freonden Oder
abilities.text: Before initiative is determined each round, roll one die. If the result is less than four, add that amount to you first attack roll of the round. If the result is four or greater, add that amount to you initiative for the round.

cards.flavor: Brought together by common cause, groups of outlaws and rogues were a common occurence in England during the Middle Ages.
cards.raw_source_text: Freonden Oder: Before initiative is determined each round, roll one die. If the result is less than four, add that amount to you first attack roll of the round. If the result is four or greater, add that amount to you initiative for the round.

cards.name: Guan Yu

abilities.name: Shen su
abilities.text: If you hit with two basic attacks in a single turn, you immediately gain +1 speed for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Born a commoner, Guan Yu rose by sheer ability to become one of the most revered military figures in Chinese history. He was instrumental in helping his friend Liu Bei, establish the Su Han kingdom in the province of Schuan.
cards.background:
Born a simple peasant farmer, Guan Yu trained as a warrior to protect all he held dear. A chance meeting with Zhang Fei and Liu Bei led to the formation of the Brothers of the Peach Orchard, a trio of vigilantes determined to restore their beloved land to glory by deposing the unjust oppressors of the Chinese people.

Wielding the Guan Dao, a formidable halberd that came to be named after him, Guan Yu will not be stopped in his quest. Guan Yu’s deification as Guan Di is a reminder to all those who oppose him in the Anachronism™ arena that they fight against an unquenchable and unconquerable nobility.
cards.raw_source_text: Shen su: If you hit with two basic attacks in a single turn, you immediately gain +1 speed for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Guan Di

abilities.name: Zhan shi zhi guang
abilities.text: Choose either attack or defense. For this Round, your rolls of that type gain +2 and your opponent's rolls of that type gain +1.

cards.flavor: Patron god of the Chinese martial arts, Guan Di symbolizes justice, loyalty, righteousness and humility, as well as military and fighting prowess.
cards.raw_source_text: Zhan shi zhi guang - Reveal: Choose either attack or defense. For this Round, your rolls of that type gain +2 and your opponent's rolls of that type gain +1.

cards.name: Qing Long Yan Yue Dao

abilities.name: Pua sui gong ji
abilities.text: After you hit with this weapon, roll two dice. If that total is greater than the defender's experience, discard one of the defender's cavalry or armor support cards.

cards.flavor: Wielding this Guan Dao, Green Dragon Sable, Guan Yu fought alongside Liu Bei and Zhang Fei during the late Eastern Han Dynasty to found one of the three kingdoms of China.
cards.raw_source_text: Pua sui gong ji: After you hit with this weapon, roll two dice. If that total is greater than the defender's experience, discard one of the defender's cavalry or armor support cards.

cards.name: Jlang Jun Zhil Kai

abilities.name: Chuan qi zhi kai
abilities.text: At the start of each round, roll two dice. If that total is even, your defense rolls this round gain +2. If you roll doubles, your attack rolls this round gain +1.

cards.flavor: Sturdy and supple, the armor of the elite of China could withstand nearly any blow from a common foot soldier's weapon.
cards.raw_source_text: Chuan qi zhi kai: At the start of each round, roll two dice. If that total is even, your defense rolls this round gain +2. If you roll doubles, your attack rolls this round gain +1.

cards.name: Tao Yuan San Jie Yi

abilities.name: Bai ba ziong di
abilities.text: Choose attack roll, defense roll, or speed. Your opponent loses all bonuses of that choosen type from card abilities for this round.

cards.flavor: Liu Bei, Guan Yu and Zhang Fei, appreciating each other's talents and goals, vowed brotherhood in Zhang Fei's peach orchard, swearing to protect each other unto death.
cards.raw_source_text: Bai ba ziong di - Reveal: Choose attack roll, defense roll, or speed. Your opponent loses all bonuses of that choosen type from card abilities for this round.

cards.name: Hua Mulan

abilities.name: Jing tong qiang
abilities.text: While you have a polearm, your attack rolls gain +1.

abilities.name: Su Di
abilities.text: At the start of the game, if there is an opposing Mongol warrior, gain 2 life and +6 experience.

cards.flavor: Famous for disguising herself as a man, Mulan distinguished herself as a soldier and was summoned to court by the emperor. Though he wished to appoint her to high office as a reward, Mulan asked only for a horse to return home.
cards.background:
Hua Mulan came to know the values of courage and filial loyalty from an early age. Refusing to let her aged father or young brother be drafted into the Chinese army, Mulan disguised herself as a man and enlisted in their place. Mulan’s prowess and bravery were unmatched. She led armies to victory and earned a commendation from the Emperor himself. Only after her military career ended was it learned that this great warrior was a woman.

A master of the qiang spear, Mulan is a deadly adversary of the Mongol hordes. Inspired to righteous retribution by the powerful Zhu Rong, Mulan is a match for any opponent, man or woman, in the Anachronism™ arena.
cards.raw_source_text:
Jing tong qiang: While you have a polearm, your attack rolls gain +1.
Su Di: At the start of the game, if there is an opposing Mongol warrior, gain 2 life and +6 experience.

cards.name: Hua Mulan

abilities.name: Jing tong qiang
abilities.text: While you have a polearm, your attack rolls gain +1.

abilities.name: Su Di
abilities.text: At the start of the game, if there is an opposing Mongol warrior, gain 2 life and +6 experience.

cards.flavor: Famous for disguising herself as a man, Mulan distinguished herself as a soldier and was summoned to court by the emperor. Though he wished to appoint her to high office as a reward, Mulan asked only for a horse to return home.
cards.background:
Hua Mulan came to know the values of courage and filial loyalty from an early age. Refusing to let her aged father or young brother be drafted into the Chinese army, Mulan disguised herself as a man and enlisted in their place. Mulan’s prowess and bravery were unmatched. She led armies to victory and earned a commendation from the Emperor himself. Only after her military career ended was it learned that this great warrior was a woman.

A master of the qiang spear, Mulan is a deadly adversary of the Mongol hordes. Inspired to righteous retribution by the powerful Zhu Rong, Mulan is a match for any opponent, man or woman, in the Anachronism™ arena.
cards.raw_source_text:
Jing tong qiang: While you have a polearm, your attack rolls gain +1.
Su Di: At the start of the game, if there is an opposing Mongol warrior, gain 2 life and +6 experience.

cards.name: Zhu Rong

abilities.name: Zheng yi de tu xi
abilities.text: Deal 3 damage to all warriors with face-up cavalry cards; deal 1 damage to all other warriors.

cards.flavor: God of fire and the Heavenly Executioner, his domain is the southern quarter of Heaven, where he rides a tiger and presides over universal order.
cards.raw_source_text: Zheng yi de tu xi - Reveal: Deal 3 damage to all warriors with face-up cavalry cards; deal 1 damage to all other warriors.

cards.name: Qiang Spear

abilities.name: Xuan feng
abilities.text: When you are attacked by an adjacent warrior, your defense rolls gain +2.

cards.flavor: Hua Mulan was a spear-master, trained in the use of the long and supple qiang, which was the primary weapon of the Chinese infantry for centuries.
cards.raw_source_text: Xuan feng: When you are attacked by an adjacent warrior, your defense rolls gain +2.

cards.name: Zhou

abilities.name: Xue xin
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 against non-Chinese warriors. Opposing cards cannot move you or change your facing.

cards.flavor: Elegant in its simplicity, the helmet of Hua Mulan's time was a simple skull-cap that provided protection from swords and thrown spears.
cards.raw_source_text: Xue xin: Your defense rolls gain +1 against non-Chinese warriors. Opposing cards cannot move you or change your facing.

cards.name: Sheng Kai De Mu Lan Hua

abilities.name: Nu xing zhi mei
abilities.text: Before a tied roll is broken by experience, you may discard this card. If you do, your roll gains +1 and you gain 2 life.

cards.flavor: The magnolia blossom is the Chinese symbol of feminine beauty.
cards.raw_source_text: Nu xing zhi mei: Before a tied roll is broken by experience, you may discard this card. If you do, your roll gains +1 and you gain 2 life.

cards.name: Jei the Tyrant

abilities.name: Ba nue zhi li
abilities.text: After you hit a warrior with a critical basic attack, take 1 damage.

cards.flavor: The last king of the Xia dynasty and originally a great king and conqueror, Jie allowed extravagance and decadance to bring about the ruin of his entire kingdom.
cards.background:
“Everything under heaven belongs to me. I am like the sun in the sky. Will the sun ever be extinguished?” Such were the words of one of China’s most powerful and reviled kings. Infamous for his love of carousing and disregard for those beneath him, Jie ordered the finest wines, treasures and women of the empire brought before him. Contemptuous of life and death alike, Jie was a living terror to both his enemies and his subjects.

Yeng Wang Ye, the great lord of death, waits to judge Jie in the afterlife. Unheeding and avaricious, Jie is driven to conquer and subjugate everything within his reach. This fervor and fury are Jie’s greatest assets in the Anachronism™ arena – they may prove too great for any warrior to defeat!
cards.raw_source_text: Ba nue zhi li: After you hit a warrior with a critical basic attack, take 1 damage.

cards.name: Yeng Wang Ye

abilities.name: Si shen zhi hu
abilities.text: After any other card is discarded, discard this card to deal 2 damage to an opponent.

cards.flavor: Greatest of the Lords of Death, Yeng Wang Ye judges all souls newly arrived in the land of the dead. He then decides whether to send to a special court for punishment or to put them back on the Wheel of Transmigration.
cards.raw_source_text: Si shen zhi hu: After any other card is discarded, discard this card to deal 2 damage to an opponent.

cards.name: Yue

abilities.name: Si lie jing shu
abilities.text: If you hit with this weapon as the first attack of your turn, discard one of the defender's non-weapon support cards.

cards.flavor: Both ceremonial and functional, the yue axe originated in southern China and was in use for thousands of years.
cards.raw_source_text: Si lie jing shu: If you hit with this weapon as the first attack of your turn, discard one of the defender's non-weapon support cards.

cards.name: Bo Xion Jla

abilities.name: Zu dang
abilities.text: If you are attacked and missed, the attacking opponent cannot attack you with their next action this turn.

cards.flavor: Lamellar armor is made by binding together metal scales into a small plate, which is then covered with lacquer to make it waterproof. These small, light plates are fastened together with cords of leather, each plate slightly overlapping the other.
cards.raw_source_text: Zu dang: If you are attacked and missed, the attacking opponent cannot attack you with their next action this turn.

cards.name: Qing Gong Palace

abilities.name: Dao ta de huang gong
abilities.text: Discard this card to play a card from any discard pile in its place. If that card has a reveal ability, use it immediately. Discard that card at the end of this round to its original discard pile.

cards.flavor: Jie's palace almost exceeded the technology of his day and, it is said, was built so high that it seemed on the verge of collapsing. It was called Qing Gong - the Tottering Palace.
cards.raw_source_text: Dao ta de huang gong - Reveal: Discard this card to play a card from any discard pile in its place. If that card has a reveal ability, use it immediately. Discard that card at the end of this round to its original discard pile.

cards.name: Sun Tzu

abilities.name: Wu xing de si lue
abilities.text: Gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Author of The Art of War, Sun Tzu's military genius has lived on for over 2 millennia.
cards.background:
One of the greatest military minds of all time, Sun Tzu literally wrote the book on military strategy. A veteran of many wars, Sun Tzu understood the value of information, of drilled troops and of apparently formless strategy. “A great general,” he said, “first wins the battle. Then he fights it”. Sun Tzu’s insights were so acute that his writings today are read and employed by people in every facet of life. On every conceivable battlefield, Sun Tzu continues to lead generals to victory.

Protected by shan wen jia mountain pattern armor, Sun Tzu once again seeks to execute his plan of invulnerability in combat. With the tactical power of the nu gong crossbow at his disposal, can any warrior stand against this most erudite of opponents in the Anachronism™ arena?
cards.raw_source_text: Wu xing de si lue - Action: Gain +1 speed on your next turn.

cards.name: Yu Huang Shang Di

abilities.name: Ming si
abilities.text: At the start of the round, if you did not make any attacks last round, your attack rolls gain +2 and you gain +4 experience for this round.

cards.flavor: Yu Huang Shang Di is the crown prince of the kingdom of Pure Felicity and Majestic Heavenly Lights and Ornaments and is known as the Pure August Jade Emperor.
cards.raw_source_text: Ming si: At the start of the round, if you did not make any attacks last round, your attack rolls gain +2 and you gain +4 experience for this round.

cards.name: Nu Gong

abilities.name: Zhong chuang shang kou
abilities.text: If you hit an opponent with this weapon, deal 1 damage to that opponent at the start of the next round.

cards.flavor: Designed as an alternative to the bow over 2,500 years ago, a crossbow required less skill to use and could be effectively deployed by newly trained troops.
cards.raw_source_text: Zhong chuang shang kou: If you hit an opponent with this weapon, deal 1 damage to that opponent at the start of the next round.

cards.name: Shan Wen Jia

abilities.name: Wan qiang di kang
abilities.text: When you roll a 1 on a die in a defense roll, that defense roll gains +3 for each 1 rolled.

cards.flavor: As each plate is similar in shape to the Chinese character for mountain, this armor was often called mountain pattern armor.
cards.raw_source_text: Wan qiang di kang: When you roll a 1 on a die in a defense roll, that defense roll gains +3 for each 1 rolled.

cards.name: Bing Fa

abilities.name: Gong qi suo bu shou ye
abilities.text: Once per attack, before your attack roll, you may choose to reduce the roll by 3. If you do, that attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor:
"The art of war is of vital importance to the State. It is a matter of life and death, a road either to safety or to ruin. Hence it is a subject of inquiry which can on no account be neglected."
-Sun Tzu, The Art of War
cards.raw_source_text: Gong qi suo bu shou ye: Once per attack, before your attack roll, you may choose to reduce the roll by 3. If you do, that attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Yueh Fei

abilities.name: Han dong shan feng
abilities.text: You gain +1 speed for each of your face-up support cards.

cards.flavor: During the Song-Dynasty, Yue Fei achieved national warrior-hero status in fighting back the invasion of the Jin Tribe. After is death, the Song Dynasty abandoned all territory north of the Huai river and fell into a state of weakness and decline.
cards.background: N/A Promo Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Han dong shan feng: You gain +1 speed for each of your face-up support cards.

cards.name: Jin Cheng Bao Guo

abilities.name: Zuo yue zhi xin
abilities.text: At the start of each round, you gain +1 speed this round for each player's initiative that matches your experience.

cards.flavor:
"Be loyal and pure to serve your country."
-Tattoo on the back of Yue Fei
cards.raw_source_text: Zuo yue zhi xin: At the start of each round, you gain +1 speed this round for each player's initiative that matches your experience.

cards.name: Dao

abilities.name: Tan xing ren
abilities.text: This weapon can attack any space in your basic attack grid at +0.

cards.flavor: Used in many styles of kung fu, the dao is often considered the root of short weapons in China.
cards.raw_source_text: Tan xing ren: This weapon can attack any space in your basic attack grid at +0.

cards.name: Hu Xion Jia

abilities.name: Gang tie zhi fu
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 until the next time you attack or use this action.

cards.flavor: Form-fitted to each user, the breastplate could deflect spear and arrow blows to the chest while remaining light enough to allow easy movement for attacking.
cards.raw_source_text: Gang tie zhi fu - Action: Your defense rolls gain +2 until the next time you attack or use this action.

cards.name: Ba Duan Jin

abilities.name: Jun shi xun lian
abilities.text: If your inspiration and warrior are the same element, you may attack with a weapon an additional time each round.

cards.flavor: The Eight-Section Brocade is an ideal lifetime exercise and was used by the troops of Yue Fie to condition themselves for battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Jun shi xun lian: If your inspiration and warrior are the same element, you may attack with a weapon an additional time each round.

cards.name: Cleopatra VII

abilities.name: 'fd
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if there is an opposing male warrior, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: Born into a crumbling Egypt slowly losing ground to the expanding Roman Empire, Cleopatra used every tool at her disposal to maintain independence and return her nation to the glory that it once held.
cards.background:
Queen of a crumbling Egypt at only 18, Cleopatra used her wits and intelligence to pit the might of Rome against itself for over twenty years. She charmed both Julius Caesar and Marcus Antonius, outlived 3 sisters and a brother, ruled as the last pharaoh of Egypt and proclaimed herself the New Isis, Queen of Kings.

With Ast, goddess of death and rebirth watching over her, Cleopatra brings her cunning and wiles into the arena. With her power mandated in the Heqa and Was Scepters she bears, Cleopatra brings a new approach to combat in Anachronism™.
cards.raw_source_text: 'fd: At the start of each round, if there is an opposing male warrior, you may move one space.

cards.name: Ast

abilities.name: Nfr
abilities.text: Opposing cards cannot discard your support cards.

abilities.name: Xwi
abilities.text: After you hit an opponent, you may discard this card to gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to the defending warrior.

cards.flavor: By tricking Ra, the great mother-goddess Ast was able to gain his powers. She was a healer and the great magician of the gods.
cards.raw_source_text:
Nfr: Opposing cards cannot discard your support cards.
Xwi: After you hit an opponent, you may discard this card to gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to the defending warrior.

cards.name: B'gsw

abilities.name: Sft m s'
abilities.text: This weapon deals +2 damage while you are in a space directly behind or diagonally behind your opponent. You may use another weapon in this game.

cards.flavor: Easily concealed in the linen and silk robes of Cleopatra's time, the dagger was a deadly close-ranged weapon, capable of striking with very little warning.
cards.raw_source_text: Sft m s': This weapon deals +2 damage while you are in a space directly behind or diagonally behind your opponent. You may use another weapon in this game.

cards.name: Golden Corselet

abilities.name: Nw
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +3. If you are hit, discard this card.

cards.flavor: After befriending Julius Caesar, Cleopatra was taken to Rome. She stayed at the house of Caesar and took an active part in the social politics of Rome until he was murdered on the steps of the senate.
cards.raw_source_text: Nw: Your defense rolls gain +3. If you are hit, discard this card.

cards.name: Heqa and Was Scepters

abilities.name: Xq't inqt
abilities.text: Before you make an attack roll, you may discard this card. If you do, and the attack hits, the attack is a critical attack.

cards.flavor: Scepters offered and represent power and protection. They also set the king apart from everyone else and conveyed his authority, both secular and religious.
cards.raw_source_text: Xq't inqt: Before you make an attack roll, you may discard this card. If you do, and the attack hits, the attack is a critical attack.

cards.name: Horemheb the Restorer

abilities.name: Pr"
abilities.text: For each Aether card in play, your attack and defense rolls gain +1 and your weapon attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Ascending to become pharaoh after Tutankhamun's death, Horemheb returned the old gods to their rightful place of worship.
cards.background:
Rising in status from a lowly officer in the Egyptian army, Horemheb served as the Great Commander of the Army under Akhenaten and Tutankhamun before becoming the last pharaoh of the 18th Dynasty. His conquests against the Hittites in Syria gave rise to his fame and legend and he ascended to the throne of Egypt through his skill and ambition, with not a drop of royal blood in his veins.

Wielding the wicked Khopesh sword, Horemheb gives praise to Horus, the god of kings on earth. In return, Horus has gifted him with an Udjat Eye, giving Horemheb great vitality and endurance. With divine protection fused to mortal ambition, who can oppose Horemheb in the Anachronism™ arena?
cards.raw_source_text: Pr": For each Aether card in play, your attack and defense rolls gain +1 and your weapon attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Horus

abilities.name: Ib 'nch
abilities.text: Once per roll, after the dice are rolled, you may deal one damage to your warrior. If you do, force any one die to be re-rolled (limit once per roll).

cards.flavor: Son of Ast (or Isis) and Osiris, Horus fought against his uncle Seth for control of Egypt. Eventually, he won and exiled Seth to the Sahara Desert, but not before his eye was destroyed by Seth.
cards.raw_source_text: Ib 'nch: Once per roll, after the dice are rolled, you may deal one damage to your warrior. If you do, force any one die to be re-rolled (limit once per roll).

cards.name: Khopesh

abilities.name: Hd 's
abilities.text: If the defender has a polearm, your attack rolls with this weapon gain +2 and this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Edged on the inside or outside curve, or both, the khopesh was a significant development in sword technology. Edged on the outside, the weapon was effective at creating slashing wounds. Edged on the inside, it was used for hacking an opponent.
cards.raw_source_text: Hd 's: If the defender has a polearm, your attack rolls with this weapon gain +2 and this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Man-High Shield

abilities.name: Wch'
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 against one handed weapons.

abilities.name: Ikr
abilities.text: At the start of any round you may discard this card. If you do, your defense rolls get -1 and you gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.flavor: With the bow dominating tactical considerations on the Egyptian battlefield, the extreme size and bulk of these shields was borne out of nesessity.
cards.raw_source_text:
Wch': Your defense rolls gain +1 against one handed weapons.
Ikr: At the start of any round you may discard this card. If you do, your defense rolls get -1 and you gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.name: Udjat Eye

abilities.name: Xk'
abilities.text: Each time you roll a 1 on any die, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: The eye of the god Hurus, Udjat means "whole" or "undamaged" and was reputed to have great healing and protective power.
cards.raw_source_text: Xk': Each time you roll a 1 on any die, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Ramses II

abilities.name: Xdb
abilities.text: If you have not made an attack this turn, make an attack with double the bonus from the appropriate attack grid. Your other attack rolls this round may not be increased by abilities or attack grids.

cards.flavor: Ramses II the Great ruled Egypt for over 60 years, building temples all along the Nile and creating statues that have stood for thousands of years.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Xdb - Action: If you have not made an attack this turn, make an attack with double the bonus from the appropriate attack grid. Your other attack rolls this round may not be increased by abilities or attack grids.

cards.name: Ra

abilities.name: Ri'
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you have less life than all opponents and a special card in play, gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to an opposing warrior. You may use another inspiration this game.

cards.flavor: Born every morning, dying every night, Ra the sun god traveled the heavens each day, fighting his great enemy Apep, the snake.
cards.raw_source_text: Ri': At the start of each round, if you have less life than all opponents and a special card in play, gain 1 life and deal 1 damage to an opposing warrior. You may use another inspiration this game.

cards.name: Minb

abilities.name: Mnmn
abilities.text: If you miss an attack with this weapon, the next hit you make with this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Infantry armed with battle axes were typically deployed after the enemy had been weakened by archers. The axe was more effective in cutting wounded or fleeing enemies to pieces than it was in breaking an intact battle line.
cards.raw_source_text: Mnmn: If you miss an attack with this weapon, the next hit you make with this weapon deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Ikm''T

abilities.name: ' r hnn
abilities.text: When you are hit, the next support card you reveal is gains +3 initiative.

abilities.name: Mn
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, your defense rolls this round gain +2.

cards.flavor: Used sparingly and only by those who could afford its great cost, scale armor was worn by attackers in chariots to protect their torso from projectile weapons.
cards.raw_source_text:
' r hnn: When you are hit, the next support card you reveal is gains +3 initiative.
Mn: At the start of each round, if you won initiative, your defense rolls this round gain +2.

cards.name: Wereryet

abilities.name: Hdhd chbn
abilities.text: At the start of the round, if you won initiative, you may move one square.

cards.flavor: Used by the Egyptian military for hundreds of years, the wereryet chariot could travel the desert with great speed and it provided an excellent platform from which to launch spears and arrows.
cards.raw_source_text: Hdhd chbn: At the start of the round, if you won initiative, you may move one square.

cards.name: Seti I

abilities.name: Mnch n
abilities.text: You may have two inspirations in play. While you do, your basic attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Seti I waged aggressive campaigns against the Hittites and Libyans, reclaiming much of Egypt's prestige abroad. He was eventually succeeded by his son, Ramses II.
cards.background:
Seti I brought stability to a devastated Egypt through conquest and construction. After the failures of the Amarna kings before him, Seti rebuilt Egypt's empire. His far-sighted goals set the foundation for all of the future Egyptian pharaohs and brought prosperity to his people.

Wearing the Khepresh war crown, bearing the the Egyptian Book of the Dead and guarded by Osiris, god of the underworld, Seti enters the Anachronism™ arena without fear of death, ready to conquer all who stand before him.
cards.raw_source_text: Mnch n: You may have two inspirations in play. While you do, your basic attacks deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Osiris

abilities.name: S'nch
abilities.text: When your are reduced to less than 1 life, instead of defeating your warrior, discard this card to set your life to 1.

cards.flavor: The god of death and the underworld, Osiris also presided over growth and vegetation. He was married to Ast (or Isis). Osiris was killed by his brother, Seth, who tricked him into a coffin before casting it into the Nile river.
cards.raw_source_text: S'nch: When your are reduced to less than 1 life, instead of defeating your warrior, discard this card to set your life to 1.

cards.name: Hornbow

abilities.name: Nis
abilities.text: You may discard a face-up non-ranged weapon you own to put this card into play from your discard pile.

abilities.name: Shw
abilities.text: This weapon deals +1 damage if you have not moved this turn.

cards.flavor: Made by joining two pieces of antelope horn with a center piece of wood, the earliest horn bows fired arrows tipped with flint or hardwood and fletched with three feathers.
cards.raw_source_text:
Nis - Action: You may discard a face-up non-ranged weapon you own to put this card into play from your discard pile.
Shw: This weapon deals +1 damage if you have not moved this turn.

cards.name: Khepresh

abilities.name: Xq't m"t
abilities.text: Opposing warriors may not use abilities on inspiration cards. An opposing warrior may spend an action to discard this card. Your cards cannot prevent this card from being discarded.

cards.flavor: This ceremonial bright crown made of cloth and adorned with golden disks was known as the blue or war-crown and was easily recognizable, even during battle.
cards.raw_source_text: Xq't m"t: Opposing warriors may not use abilities on inspiration cards. An opposing warrior may spend an action to discard this card. Your cards cannot prevent this card from being discarded.

cards.name: Reu Nu Pert Em Hru

abilities.name: Bswt
abilities.text: Warriors cannot gain life.

abilities.name: Snb
abilities.text: All other warriors with more than their printed life are reduced to their printed life.

cards.flavor: The Egyptian Book of the Dead was written on papyrus. It was a collection of mystical spells and formulae that ensured an Egyptians' passage into the afterlife.
cards.raw_source_text:
Bswt: Warriors cannot gain life.
Snb - Reveal: All other warriors with more than their printed life are reduced to their printed life.

cards.name: Srqt. The Scorpion King

abilities.name: Sh'w r wrt
abilities.text: Once per game, after you make a roll, you may change one of the dice to a six.

cards.flavor: Legendary even to the pharaohs of the old kingdoms, Srqt conquered all of Egypt centuries before the great dynasties
cards.background:
Nearly legendary even to the people of the Old Kingdom, Srqt ruled all of Egypt before the dynasties of the pharaohs. He led his armies to conquer all of the surrounding nations and his subjects lived in a time of prosperity and wealth.

Wielding spear and mace, Srqt brings his legacy of power and skill to the Anachronism™ arena. He is once again ready for battle, this time to face off against the upstart warriors who have followed in his wake and emerge victorious.
cards.raw_source_text: Sh'w r wrt: Once per game, after you make a roll, you may change one of the dice to a six.

cards.name: Khnum

abilities.name: M' 'n
abilities.text: Your next attack deals +1 damage.

abilities.name: ' r hnn
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +2 against warriors without a face-up inspiration.

cards.flavor: The progenitor god of Egypt, he created all of the gods and the lands. He ruled the waters, both of the rivers and of the sea.
cards.raw_source_text:
M' 'n - Reveal: Your next attack deals +1 damage.
' r hnn: Your attack rolls gain +2 against warriors without a face-up inspiration.

cards.name: Nsywt

abilities.name: Qm'
abilities.text: When you are hit by a non-adjacent opponent, deal 1 damage to the attacker. You may use another weapon.

cards.flavor: One of the earliest types of weapon invented by man, the nsywt, a javelin, was little more than a sharpened stick in its earliest incarnation.
cards.raw_source_text:
Qm': When you are hit by a non-adjacent opponent, deal 1 damage to the attacker.
You may use another weapon.

cards.name: Nsywt

abilities.name: Qm'
abilities.text: When you are hit by a non-adjacent opponent, deal 1 damage to the attacker. You may use another weapon.

cards.flavor: One of the earliest types of weapon invented by man, the nsywt, a javelin, was little more than a sharpened stick in its earliest incarnation.
cards.raw_source_text:
Qm': When you are hit by a non-adjacent opponent, deal 1 damage to the attacker.
You may use another weapon.

cards.name: Ikm Mskw

abilities.name: Ysh'
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have more life than the attacking warrior.

abilities.name: Dnd
abilities.text: At the start any round, you may discard this card deal 1 damage to your warrior. If you do, your attacks this round deal +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Made from thick antelope hide and held together with sinew and bone, this seemingly makeshift armor offered protection from early piercing weapons.
cards.raw_source_text:
Ysh': Your defense rolls gain +1 while you have more life than the attacking warrior.
Dnd: At the start any round, you may discard this card deal 1 damage to your warrior. If you do, your attacks this round deal +1 damage.

cards.name: Mace of the Scorpion King

abilities.name: Sncht nbt
abilities.text: When you make an attack roll, if any of the dice are a 6, the attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: This mace was created for ceremonial duties and to record the actions of Srqt. It told the stories of this conquests and of his patronage, as king, of agricultural development.
cards.raw_source_text: Sncht nbt: When you make an attack roll, if any of the dice are a 6, the attack deals +1 damage.

cards.name: Attila the Hun

abilities.name: Noyarqual
abilities.text: After any player attacks, your attack rolls this round gain +1 to a maximum of +6.

cards.flavor: Merciless leader of the Huns against the Romans, Visigoths and all who stood before them, Attila was known as the Scourge of God.
cards.background:
Attila the Hun began his reign over western Asia and eastern Europe in the fourth century AD. Drawn from Mongol stock, the Huns were forced westward by failing crops. Ruthless and without mercy, Attila saw to the devastation of many Gallic and Balkan cities. The “Scourge of God”, as he was known by a terrified Christian populace, led one of the most heavily armed forces of the era and used it with brutal and uncompromising efficiency.

Attila the Hun bears the Sword of Mars, which is said to make its wielder undefeatable on the field of battle. Driven on by the raging god Nemere, no opponent can hope to face Attila in the Anachronism™ arena without knowing the true meaning of fear.
cards.raw_source_text: Noyarqual: After any player attacks, your attack rolls this round gain +1 to a maximum of +6.

cards.name: Nemere

abilities.name: Qaugiqu salkin
abilities.text: At the start of each round, choose a direction. At the start of each player's turn, move their warrior one space in the chosen direction if possible.

cards.flavor: Nemere is the Hunnish god of war and the raging southern wind.
cards.raw_source_text: Qaugiqu salkin: At the start of each round, choose a direction. At the start of each player's turn, move their warrior one space in the chosen direction if possible.

cards.name: Sword of Mars

abilities.name: Amin-tu kurku qoorlal
abilities.text: If you have less than 3 life and hit your opponent with this weapon, that opponent is reduced to one life after the attack.

cards.flavor: Found by Attila on a forgotten battlefield, he claimed it to be the invincible sword of Mars, Roman god of war, and took it for himself.
cards.raw_source_text: Amin-tu kurku qoorlal: If you have less than 3 life and hit your opponent with this weapon, that opponent is reduced to one life after the attack.

cards.name: Duugla

abilities.name: Mon cinar-un tusqual
abilities.text: Once per round, after making a defense roll, you may set the roll equal to the attacking warrior's initiative for this round.

cards.flavor: Worn for centuries among the nomadic barbarians of central and western Asia, helmets of this type protected their wearers against the depredations of both battle and climate.
cards.raw_source_text: Mon cinar-un tusqual: Once per round, after making a defense roll, you may set the roll equal to the attacking warrior's initiative for this round.

cards.name: Gäitäy Geprech

abilities.name: Emiyel
abilities.text: When an opponent's attacks you and misses, they take 1 damage if their experience is less than 2. Otherwise, they get experience -2 for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: In the reign of Attila, the Huns became the terror of the world, slaughtering cities and destroying civilizations.
cards.raw_source_text: Emiyel: When an opponent's attacks you and misses, they take 1 damage if their experience is less than 2. Otherwise, they get experience -2 for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Gengis Khan

abilities.name: Erkesiyeku ary-a
abilities.text: Your attack rolls are +1 while attacking an opponent without a cavalry card.

cards.flavor: Genghis Khan, the inspired leader of a tribe of steppe nomads, led his people to establish the Mongol Empire, the largest land empire in the history of mankind.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Erkesiyeku ary-a: Your attack rolls are +1 while attacking an opponent without a cavalry card.

cards.name: Gengis Khan

abilities.name: Erkesiyeku ary-a
abilities.text: Your attack rolls are +1 while attacking an opponent without a cavalry card.

cards.flavor: Genghis Khan, the inspired leader of a tribe of steppe nomads, led his people to establish the Mongol Empire, the largest land empire in the history of mankind.
cards.background: N/A Starter Warrior
cards.raw_source_text: Erkesiyeku ary-a: Your attack rolls are +1 while attacking an opponent without a cavalry card.

cards.name: Koekki Moengke Tengri

abilities.name: Eguride-yin koke tngri
abilities.text: After you make an attack roll, you may discard this card to set the attack roll equal to your experience.

cards.flavor: Eternal Blue Heaven is the foremost of the spirits of heaven.
cards.raw_source_text: Eguride-yin koke tngri: After you make an attack roll, you may discard this card to set the attack roll equal to your experience.

cards.name: Alman Sukh

abilities.name: Cabciqu
abilities.text: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage when attacking an opponent without a face-up cavalry card.

cards.flavor: Each mongol warrior carried a battle axe (alman sukh), a curved sword (tomor schimitar), a lance (zhad), and two recurved bows (num mor and num tom).
cards.raw_source_text: Cabciqu: Attacks with this weapon deal +1 damage when attacking an opponent without a face-up cavalry card.

cards.name: Khurim

abilities.name: Kuiten-I sergeileku
abilities.text: Your opponent has no attack bonuses for this round.

cards.flavor: The design of the khurim jacket has been virtually unchanged for over a thousand years. It is traditionally worn by horsemen to protect them from the fierce Mongolian winters.
cards.raw_source_text: Kuiten-I sergeileku - Reveal: Your opponent has no attack bonuses for this round.

cards.name: Zhad

abilities.name: Am onggereku
abilities.text: Once per round, you may roll 2 dice. If the total is greater than an adjacent warrior's initiative for this round, deal 2 damage to that warrior or 3 damage if you have a face-up cavalry card. You may use another special card in this game.

cards.flavor: After using num mor and num tom to soften the enemy ranks, the zhad was employed to break the formations of opposing foot soldiers.
cards.raw_source_text: Am onggereku - Action: Once per round, you may roll 2 dice. If the total is greater than an adjacent warrior's initiative for this round, deal 2 damage to that warrior or 3 damage if you have a face-up cavalry card. You may use another special card in this game.

cards.name: Khutulun

abilities.name: Itegel
abilities.text: After you are missed by a basic attack, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: The niece of Kublai Khan, Khutulun never gave her hand in marriage, insisting that each suitor best her in a wrestling match to win her affections.
cards.background:
The niece of Kublai Khan, Khutulun promised to marry any man that could best her in a wrestling challenge. Each challenger had to make a bet of one hundred horses on the outcome of the match. If the challenger won, Khutulun would marry him. If not, the horses were hers to keep. She was never beaten, remained unmarried and amassed over ten thousand horses from hapless suitors. She didn’t think it too many.

Khutulun’s gutal boots root her to the earth, while the holy mountain, Burqan-Qaldun, is a metaphor for her unconquerable spirit. Whether or not Khutulun’s opponent in the Anachronism™ arena intends to marry her, they had better bring one hundred horses with them and had better expect to lose them.
cards.raw_source_text: Itegel: After you are missed by a basic attack, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Burqan-Qaldun

abilities.name: Tuil-un qurduca
abilities.text: At the start of each round, all warriors whose initiative values for this round are odd gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.flavor: The sacred mountain has seen the launch of epic invasions that have conquered nations from one end of Asia to the other.
cards.raw_source_text: Tuil-un qurduca: At the start of each round, all warriors whose initiative values for this round are odd gain +1 speed for this round.

cards.name: Khutga

abilities.name: Konggen boloqu
abilities.text: You may use another weapon in this game.

abilities.name: Amua ezex
abilities.text: Discard this card and gain 2 life.

cards.flavor: Although mainly used for personal defense, the khutga played an important role in the cermonial lives of the Mongols.
cards.raw_source_text:
Konggen boloqu: You may use another weapon in this game.
Amua ezex - Action: Discard this card and gain 2 life.

cards.name: Gutal

abilities.name: Kodelgegentu cinar
abilities.text: After you gain life from another card, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: Boots, as with all Mongol clothing, were heavily constructed to withstand long journies and arduous conditions.
cards.raw_source_text: Kodelgegentu cinar: After you gain life from another card, you may move one space.

cards.name: 100 Aduu Bootsoo

abilities.name: Uridcilan medegsen teguskel
abilities.text: If you are the first player to deal damage after this card is revealed, your basic attacks deal +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Each man who chose to vie for the hand of Khutulum had to bet 100 steeds on the outcome of their wrestling match. No man ever walked away with a wife or his horses.
cards.raw_source_text: Uridcilan medegsen teguskel: If you are the first player to deal damage after this card is revealed, your basic attacks deal +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.name: Kubulai Khan

abilities.name: Jabsartabal labtai ilaydan-a
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each of the defender's support cards that are a different culture than the defender.

cards.flavor: Grandson of Genghis Khan, Kublai established the foundations of a grand empire in China and Mongolia. He hosted Marco Polo, who returned to Europe with amazing stories of the mysterious East.
cards.background:
Grandson of the great Temujin, Kublai ruled his great empire from his palaces in China. Kublai proved his military acumen and might against his brother and uncle before securing his kingdom, defeating the Sung Dynasty, and invading both Japan and Java.

During the course of his reign, Kublai defeated kingdoms, rebellions, and countless other enemies. His military genius now comes to the Anachronism™ arena, where he will prove to the world that the Mongol Empire is alive and strong.
cards.raw_source_text: Jabsartabal labtai ilaydan-a: Your attack rolls gain +1 for each of the defender's support cards that are a different culture than the defender.

cards.name: Xanadu

abilities.name: Debajan
abilities.text: At the end of each round, if you dealt damage to another warrior, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: During Kublai's reign, the summer capitol of the great Mongol empire was Xanadu, while the winter capitol was Dadu, which is modern-day Beijing.
cards.raw_source_text: Debajan: At the end of each round, if you dealt damage to another warrior, gain 1 life.

cards.name: Tomor Scimitar

abilities.name: Dutaydal-i ni baicayaqu
abilities.text: Once per game, before you make an attack roll, gain a damage bonus equal to your opponent's largest defense bonus +1.

cards.flavor: Curved to slice through men while riding past, the tomor scimitar was used by mounted warriors for man-to-man fighting once ranks broke.
cards.raw_source_text: Dutaydal-i ni baicayaqu: Once per game, before you make an attack roll, gain a damage bonus equal to your opponent's largest defense bonus +1.

cards.name: Tsamts Torgo

abilities.name: Oncoyoi qamayalalta
abilities.text: You may discard this card before an attack roll is made. If you do, the attack deals one less damage and the attacker may not use any card abilities for this attack.

cards.flavor: Originating in China, these undergarments made the rigors of distance riding in full battle gear bearable and offered unparalleled protection against arrow wounds.
cards.raw_source_text: Oncoyoi qamayalalta: You may discard this card before an attack roll is made. If you do, the attack deals one less damage and the attacker may not use any card abilities for this attack.

cards.name: Num Tom

abilities.name: Morgoldungen
abilities.text: You and a non-adjacent warrior each roll two dice. If your total is greater than thier experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior. If their total is less than your experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior.

cards.flavor: For use at great range, the num tom was the first weapon used in any Mongol cavalry attack, arcing arrows above the open land between the Mongols and their doomed opponents.
cards.raw_source_text: Morgoldungen - Reveal: You and a non-adjacent warrior each roll two dice. If your total is greater than thier experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior. If their total is less than your experience, deal 1 damage to their warrior.

cards.name: Subedei

abilities.name: Sain-tai tal-a-yi eriku
abilities.text: Once per round, when making an attack roll, you may re-roll one die of the attack roll. If the new result of the die is the same as the old result, deal one damage to the defending warrior.

cards.flavor: Genghis Khan's most brillian general, the renowned Subedei crushed the Kwarazmian Empire and moved on to subjugate the Russians and the Cumans.
cards.background:
Genghis Khan’s greatest general was responsible for more than sixty successful campaigns in Eastern Europe. A peerless general whose achievements stand out even against the likes of Alexander the Great, Subedei employed tactics and strategies that were decades ahead of his time. Without the brilliance of Subedei, Genghis Khan could never have established and ruled over the largest land empire the world has ever known.

Dedicated to the Yeke Mongghol Ulus, the great Mongol Empire, Subedei brings a different kind of combat to the Anachronism™ arena. Riding a mangudai steed, trained in the feigned flight tactic of the Mongols, Subedei’s opponents will quickly find themselves outmaneuvered and soundly defeated by this most cunning of warriors.
cards.raw_source_text: Sain-tai tal-a-yi eriku: Once per round, when making an attack roll, you may re-roll one die of the attack roll. If the new result of the die is the same as the old result, deal one damage to the defending warrior.

cards.name: Yeke Mongghol Ulus

abilities.name: Kucurkeg turimekei ulus
abilities.text: If you you have a face-up ranged weapon and you won initiative this round, move an opposing warrior one space.

cards.flavor: The Mongol Empire is the largest recorded land empire in the history of mankind. It stretched from the Pacific Ocean to the Black Sea and was created from a single family of Mongol nomads in just 25 years.
cards.raw_source_text: Kucurkeg turimekei ulus - Action: If you you have a face-up ranged weapon and you won initiative this round, move an opposing warrior one space.

cards.name: Num Mor

abilities.name: Qaralta-ban todolaqu
abilities.text: After an opponent moves for the second time in a turn, you may immediately make a basic attack against that warrior at +0.

cards.flavor: While harrying enemy ranks, the num mor could be used with speed and efficiency from horseback while the warrior used his legs to control his well-trained steed.
cards.raw_source_text: Qaralta-ban todolaqu: After an opponent moves for the second time in a turn, you may immediately make a basic attack against that warrior at +0.

cards.name: Khatangu

abilities.name: Caexu
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 if your experience is odd.

abilities.name: Tailqu
abilities.text: When you take damage from something other than an attack, you may discard this card to prevent the damage.

cards.flavor: A woven shirt covered with a layer of hardened leather plates, the khatangu was flexible to ease horseback riding while still providing protection from arrows and upward-directed spears.
cards.raw_source_text:
Caexu: Your defense rolls gain +1 if your experience is odd. 
Tailqu: When you take damage from something other than an attack, you may discard this card to prevent the damage.

cards.name: Mangudai Steed

abilities.name: Qayurmay-iyar bayuju ogku
abilities.text: Once per turn, after you move out of a space in an opposing warrior's attack grid, you may immediately attack that opponent with one of your ranged weapons at +0.

cards.flavor: The selection process for the elite Mangudai unit was grueling and dangerous. All of its members were superbly disciplined and trained, the perfect spearhead force for the attacking Mongol armies.
cards.raw_source_text: Qayurmay-iyar bayuju ogku: Once per turn, after you move out of a space in an opposing warrior's attack grid, you may immediately attack that opponent with one of your ranged weapons at +0.
